content: WebRTC data channel game input pattern: event-driven is standard (onmessage callback). For high-frequency sensor data, unreliable channels (ordered=false, maxRetransmits=0) give UDP semantics — stale packets drop rather than queue. Polling anti-pattern: do not poll, receive events. AirConsole: 'device_motion' opt on instantiation with interval in ms — developer chooses frequency. Architecture: one data channel per input type or multiplex with message type header. Binary encoding (ArrayBuffer) preferred over JSON at 60Hz. Labels on channels improve debuggability. Both polling and event-driven SDKs should be exposed: getPlayerInput() for polling pattern within game loop, on('imuUpdate') for reactive pattern.
